commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Control UI usage-page agent selector sourced from configured agents, threads `usageAgentId` through `sessions.usage`, and adds controller/view tests.

PR surface: Source +42, Tests +109. Total +151 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main omits `agentId` from the Web UI usage request while the current backend scopes omitted `agentId` to the configured default agent. I did not run a live browser/gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for concrete code fixes and real behavior proof; automation should not take over because the external PR still lacks contributor-supplied live proof.

target.title: fix(ui): add agentId selector to usage page for multi-agent session scoping
target.body:
## Summary

Add `usageAgentId` state and an agent selector dropdown to the Control UI usage page so operators can explicitly scope `sessions.usage` requests to a specific agent. When no agent is selected, the backend's existing scoped default applies.

The v2026.5.17 fix (6a12c6f79915 / #81386) properly scoped `sessions.usage` lookups by `agentId`. The Web UI was never updated to send `agentId`, so non-main agent sessions were invisible on the usage page. This PR adds the missing frontend support without changing the backend security boundary.

Agent dropdown options are populated from the configured agents list (`agents.list` API), with a fallback to session-derived agent IDs when the list is unavailable. The currently selected agent is always preserved in the dropdown.

## Behavior addressed

The Web UI usage page `loadUsage` never sent `agentId` in `sessions.usage` requests. The backend correctly defaults omitted `agentId` to the configured default agent. This PR adds a client-side agent selector dropdown populated from configured agents so operators can choose which agent's usage to query.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Web UI usage page had no way to request non-default-agent sessions from the `sessions.usage` API. The agent dropdown was populated from already-returned sessions, making non-main agents invisible.

**Real environment tested:** pnpm test on macOS 26, Node 24, openclaw checkout at commit aaded93407 on branch fix/usage-agent-filter-87132.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** All 66 tests pass across 6 test suites:
- Backend: 29/29 pass (unchanged from main, scoped default preserved)
- Frontend `usage.node.test.ts`: 11/11 pass — includes new tests:
  - `includes agentId in sessions.usage request when usageAgentId is set` — verifies `agentId: "opus"` is in the payload
  - `omits agentId from sessions.usage request when usageAgentId is not set` — verifies no `agentId` key when `usageAgentId` is undefined
- Frontend `usage.test.ts`: 3/3 pass — includes new view tests:
  - `renders agent selector dropdown from configured allAgentIds` — verifies dropdown renders with "main" and "opus" options from `allAgentIds` prop
  - `shows selected usageAgentId in agent selector` — verifies dropdown preselected value matches `usageAgentId`
- Frontend `app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`: 23/23 pass

**Observed result after fix:** The agent selector dropdown is populated from `agents.list` (configured agents), not from session data. When `usageAgentId` is set, `loadUsage` includes `agentId` in the `sessions.usage` payload. When not set, `agentId` is omitted and the backend's scoped default applies. The view renders all configured agent IDs as dropdown options and preselects the current `usageAgentId`.

**What was not tested:** Full browser-based end-to-end testing with a live multi-agent gateway was not performed. The agent selector dropdown UX interaction (selecting an agent triggers reload via `onAgentChange`) was verified through unit test coverage and code review.

Fixes #87132

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
